name: Wild Purple Sprout
terroir: High altitude (1500-3600m), persistent fog, wild old-growth tea forests, trees often 300-500+ years old
cultivar: Camellia assamica var. dehongensis (wild purple mutation, high anthocyanin)
flavor_profile: A striking wild tea with a faintly purple liquor from its anthocyanin-rich buds. The flavor opens with sweet sage and elderflower, then builds into fruity sweetness — blueberry, apricot — with a sharp pine-needle finish that lingers. More structured and assertive than standard Ya Bao, with herbal depth.

brewing.tips: The purple buds can handle higher temperatures than most white teas. Watch for astringency on early steeps — if it bites, drop the temp to 85°C. The liquor will have a subtle violet tint from the anthocyanins.
